race.name: Gran Fondo Suisse
race.tagline: 150km of Vaudois Alps brutality with 3,700m+ that crushes souls like a Grand Tour stage.

vitals.field_size: Growing amateur peloton, UCI qualifiers draw serious climbers but exact numbers unpublished
vitals.registration: UCI World Series qualifier entry, costs unspecified but includes pro-level organization and combined gravel weekend vibe
vitals.prize_purse: UCI qualifier prestige over cash, glory for top age-groupers
vitals.aid_stations: Swiss efficiency on spectator-friendly alpine passes
vitals.cutoff_time: Flexible for qualifiers, climbers get time
distance_options: Granfondo: 93mi, 12,139ft — UCI qualifier for 19-59 men/19-49 women over iconic Vaud passes
Mediofondo: 60-66mi, 8,579ft — Qualifier for 60+ men/50+ women with key climbs
Time Trial: 4.6mi, 1,532ft — Climber's 7.4km sufferfest to Col de la Croix
Shorter: 45mi variant — Mosses and Croix essentials

climate.description: July at 1,300m brings 20-25°C highs for daytime suffering, cooling evenings perfect for recovery beers. Afternoon showers or alpine thunderstorms can turn descents treacherous. Pack layers for Vaudois microclimates.

terrain.primary: Grand Tour-worthy alpine roads and offroad
terrain.surface: Paved HC climbs mix with 10% offroad gravel crushers and wooded singletracks. Iconic passes like Croix demand big-ring power early, brutal steeps later. Spectator heaven with accessible viewpoints.
features: Col de la Croix summit finish

gravel_god_rating.tier_note: T2 for emerging UCI powerhouse punching above young weight with Grand Tour climbs near HQ. Prestige and elevation scream elite but youth caps field depth. Climbers' paradise in making.

logistics.explanation: Geneva Airport and rack railway make access dreamy for Euro peloton. UCI HQ proximity screams legitimacy. Combined gravel weekend? Genius packaging, though Swiss prices sting wallet.

length.explanation: 150km hits epic without Maratona excess—pure alpine density. Medio options smart for mortals. Time trial bonus turns weekend into climber's camp heaven.

technicality.explanation: Offroad 10% and 15% ramps spice roads nicely, but mostly tarmac for road hammers. Woods add grit without full gravel chaos. Balanced for Swiss precision.

elevation.explanation: 3,700m+ over Vaud passes is Grand Tour savage—Croix, Pillon, Mosses obliterate quads. Density crushes harder than distance suggests. Pure vert porn.

climate.explanation: July mildness tempts, but alpine storms brew mid-climb drama. 20-25C perfect till thunder gods intervene. Risk-reward amps suffering.

altitude.explanation: 1,778m Croix tests hypoxia without Etna brutality. 1,300m base acclimates fast. High enough for thin air pain, low enough for mortals.

adventure.explanation: Vaudois unknown gems, Hongrin wilds, offroad surprises—feels exploratory despite roads. Alps grandeur unmatched. Weekend with gravel doubles immersion.

prestige.explanation: UCI World Series qualifier near HQ carries weight fast. Young but Tour de Suisse DNA elevates. Rising star for ageless domestiques.

race_quality.explanation: Pro-stage stature, spectator-friendly, flawless Swiss execution. Time trial innovation killer. Best new fonda in years.

experience.explanation: Majestic Alps, summit finishes, resort vibe—post-race apres unmatched. Feels pro without pro pressure. Bucket-list stunner.

community.explanation: Amateur UCI qualifiers build buzz, but field size mystery. Gravel combo grows scene. Needs vets to solidify cult status.

field_depth.explanation: Qualifiers lure contenders, but unproven numbers. Age-group focus quality over depth. Potential for stacked starts.

value.explanation: UCI glory and scenery justify cost—assuming sane fees. Dual-event weekend maximizes bang. Swiss tax hurts but worth it.

expenses.explanation: Swiss Alps = premium pricing, but Geneva access offsets flights. Resort lodging flexible. Budget wisely or weep.

biased_opinion.summary: Gran Fondo Suisse delivers Grand Tour pain in Vaud's hidden Alps—150km shredding Croix, Pillon, Mosses with 3,700m that humble even pros. UCI pedigree near HQ plus gravel twin makes ultimate Swiss weekend. Offroad kicks and time trial genius elevate beyond standard fonda drudgery. Young event punches like veteran; if you climb, this owns your soul.
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Elite climbers: drop everything for 2026 Majestics. Mortals train Medio hard. Skip if flats are your jam.

final_verdict.one_liner: Vaud Alps' brutal UCI gem redefines fondo suffering.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you're a climber chasing World Series glory and 3,700m badges. Skip if gravel-phobic or budget-tight—thunder and francs bite hard.
final_verdict.alternatives: Maratona dles Dolomites for Italian vert fest, Haute Route Alps for multi-day pain, Sellaronda for Dolomite pixie dust.

logistics.airport: Geneva (GVA) closest, 90min drive/train east of lake. Aigle rack railway scenic AF from Bex.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Villars resort books fast—aim chalets or hotels 6mo out. Shoulder season cheap if flexible.
logistics.food: Swiss fondue/raclette recovery heaven, pre-race muesli discipline. Resort dining solid, markets stocked.
logistics.parking: Ample resort lots, car-free start zone likely.

history.founder: UCI-endorsed local organizers leveraging Villars cycling heritage
history.origin_story: Born 2019 near UCI Aigle HQ to mimic Tour de Romandie/Suisse stage finishes for amateurs. First edition eyed 2020 over Vaud passes. Quick UCI Gran Fondo Series riser with pro-caliber courses. 2024 refresh added Alpes Vadoises fire. Now Majestics for 2026 immortality.
history.reputation: Rising brutally fast as climber's UCI must. Swiss polish meets alpine savagery. Word spreading among vert junkies.

course_description.character: Grand Tour mountain stage disguised as fondo—starts Villars, warms with Croix/Pillon double, dives Hongrin low at 690m via Mittelberg wilds. Second half Mosses/Forclaz punch back to Croix summit pain. Offroad woods and 15% ramps mix paved power. 150km feels eternal, spectator vistas epic.

suffering_zones.label: Col de la Croix Warm-Up
suffering_zones.desc: First HC bite at 1,778m sets tone—steady 6-13% to ski station views. Legs awaken brutally early. Iconic opener dazes flats lovers.

suffering_zones.label: Hongrin Valley Plunge
suffering_zones.desc: Lowest point 690m after Mittelberg—false flat traps before Mosses surge. Wild serene contrast to prior pain. Mental reset turns trap.

suffering_zones.label: Final Croix Death
suffering_zones.desc: 12km @6.5% avg post-Mosses/Forclaz, 15% crushers. Summit finish shreds survivors. Glory or walk-up.

course_description.signature_challenge: Triple Croix motif—opener, mid-link, summit killer—embodies Vaudois relentless vert. 7.4km TT variant pure climber duel. Grand Tour DNA in amateur flesh.

non_negotiables.why: Simulates 150km vert bomb—builds quad armor for 15% hell. Test 3,700m days or crack on Croix. No shortcuts in Vaud.
Steadies 6.5% finales amid fatigue. Lactic tolerance decides summit survivors. Swiss Alps punish power meters.

youtube_data.search_query: Gran Fondo Suisse gran fondo cycling Villars-sur-Ollon

videos.title: TdS 2023 - Schlussaufstieg Villars-sur-Ollon
videos.description:
Mathias Frank hat den Schlussaufstieg der 3. Etappe nach Villars-sur-Ollon bereits befahren. Seine Einschätzung dazu im Video.

Kamera & Schnitt: Jan Mühlethaler | roja-films

videos.title: Sur les routes du Gran Fondo UCI Suisse
videos.description:
Cette année le Tour de France passe en Suisse, l'occasion de faire les cols des Mosses et de La Croix. Nous avons fait en groupe le tour classique des alpes vaudoises avec le col du Pillon en plus! Magnifique journée dans les alpes du canton voisin! Merci à Alain et à @myvaud  pour l'invitation.


le tour complet sur mon strava: https://www.strava.com/activities/7356025784


Mon compte insta: https://www.instagram.com/valaiscyclisme/

Le site du MyVaud: https://www.region-du-leman.ch/fr/
Gran Fondo UCI Suisse:  https://www.ucigranfondosuisse.ch

00:00 Introduction
00:40 Col de La Croix
03:40 Col du Pillon
05:44 Gstaad
07:21 Château-d'Oex
07:55 Col des Mosses
10:25 Remontée du Col de La Croix
tags: Je fais du vélo en Suisse
videos.curation_reason: Course preview covering the classic alpine route including key climbs like Col des Mosses and Col de la Croix.

videos.title: UCI Gravel Suisse Alps - First 55km Uncut
videos.description:
Here is the uncut version of the UCI Gravel World Series: Gravel Suisse in Villars with data overlays. Racing through the amazing Swiss Alps. It was a tough race, but so beautiful at the same time. Join me for this unique experience in an incredible setting.

I will upload the full story of this race later on this channel, as that is taking some time to edit. Stay tuned ;)

Partners of the channel:
🌳Urban Sustainability Projects🌳: https://www.alta-sv.nl
🧑‍🎓Coaching voor advocaten en juristen👩‍🎓: https://www.honigcoaching.nl
👨‍💼Security- | Travel- | Estate managent👨‍💼: https://mkaservices.nl/
🌏Your Reliable Trading Partner🌏: https://dhmobility.com

Feel free to follow me on Strava: https://www.strava.com/athletes/flyinggravelman
Contact: HesTech.FGM@gmail.com

This race is part of the UCI World Gravel series and I race in the age group 18-35.

Link to route or GPX: https://www.komoot.com/nl-nl/tour/1686293222
videos.curation_reason: Recent first-person race footage with data overlays showing actual race conditions in the Swiss Alps with good view count.
